title: Epistemology
description: How we know - knowledge confidence and provenance

canonical-dsl-pattern.name: Canonical DSL Preservation
canonical-dsl-pattern.description: NEVER translate MoneyWorks terminology - preserve exact field names to maintain semantic integrity
canonical-dsl-pattern.rationale: MoneyWorks uses GST for ALL tax types globally. Preserving exact terminology prevents semantic drift and maintains API consistency.

field-discovery-pattern.name: XML-based Field Discovery
field-discovery-pattern.description: Since TSV exports lack headers, use XML exports to discover field names and structure
field-discovery-pattern.rationale: MoneyWorks TSV format has no header row, making XML the only way to discover field names and order dynamically.

client-caching-pattern.name: LRU Client Caching
client-caching-pattern.description: Cache MoneyWorks clients with TTL-based cleanup to avoid recreation overhead

multi-format-export-pattern.name: Multiple Export Formats
multi-format-export-pattern.description: Support 4 export formats for different use cases: compact (arrays), compact-headers, full (objects), schema (enriched)

formats.schema: Objects with field metadata for LLM understanding

middleware-pipeline-pattern.name: Elysia Middleware Chain
middleware-pipeline-pattern.description: Ordered middleware chain: requestId → errorHandler → logging → i18n → auth

branded-types-pattern.name: Branded Type Safety
branded-types-pattern.description: Use branded types for domain primitives to prevent misuse (YYYYMMDD, AccountCode, etc.)
branded-types-pattern.benefit: Compile-time prevention of passing wrong string types (e.g., can't pass ISO date where YYYYMMDD expected)

table-registry-registration-pattern.name: TableRegistry Registration Pattern
table-registry-registration-pattern.description: API controllers MUST be registered in TableRegistry.register() to be available via REST API - registration is NOT optional
table-registry-registration-pattern.criticalImportance: HIGH - determines whether entity is 'available' or stuck in 'upcoming' status
examples: ProductController NOT registered (US-001) → Product still in 'upcoming' list
evidence: US-002: Account in 'available' array after registration
US-001: Product in 'upcoming' array without registration
table-registry-registration-pattern.antipattern: Creating controller without registering it - makes entity inaccessible via API

5-file-canonical-pattern.name: 5-File Canonical Entity Structure
5-file-canonical-pattern.description: Canonical entities follow enums → types → fields → validators → index structure for consistency

structure.types.ts: TypeScript interfaces with exact MW field names
structure.fields.ts: Field metadata (dataType, maxLength, description, validation rules)

5-file-canonical-pattern.rationale: Consistent structure improves discoverability and reduces cognitive load when working across entities

repository-mock-mode-pattern.name: Repository Mock Mode Pattern
repository-mock-mode-pattern.description: Repositories support MONEYWORKS_MOCK_MODE env variable with realistic sample data for autonomous testing
repository-mock-mode-pattern.rationale: Enables autonomous testing without live MoneyWorks connection

specialized-repository-methods-pattern.name: Specialized Repository Query Methods
specialized-repository-methods-pattern.description: Repositories provide domain-specific query methods beyond generic CRUD for better DX
specialized-repository-methods-pattern.rationale: Domain-specific methods improve code readability and reduce cognitive load compared to generic filters
evidence: US-002: AccountRepository has 11 specialized query methods

9-step-entity-implementation-pattern.name: 9-Step Entity Implementation Checklist
9-step-entity-implementation-pattern.description: Complete entity implementation requires 9 files across 3 packages: canonical (3), data (3), api (2), plus registry update
9-step-entity-implementation-pattern.rationale: Ensures complete backend entity implementation with no missed exports or registrations
evidence: ACCT-001: All 9 steps followed, Account available in API

triple-export-registration-pattern.name: Triple Export Registration for Repositories
triple-export-registration-pattern.description: New repositories require export in 3 locations: repositories/index.ts, main index.ts, AND both factory functions
triple-export-registration-pattern.antipattern: Forgetting any of the 3 locations causes 'Module has no exported member' errors
evidence: ACCT-001: Initial typecheck failed until all 3 locations updated

repository-query-categories-pattern.name: Repository Query Method Categories
repository-query-categories-pattern.description: Organize repository queries into 5 categories for consistent API design
repository-query-categories-pattern.rationale: Taxonomy helps ensure consistent naming and comprehensive coverage across entities

bitwise-role-search-pattern.name: MoneyWorks Bitwise Role Search Syntax
bitwise-role-search-pattern.description: MoneyWorks search supports bitwise operations using (Field&#xx)!=0 syntax for efficient role-based filtering
bitwise-role-search-pattern.syntax: (Role&#xx)!=0 where xx is hex role bit
bitwise-role-search-pattern.rationale: Enables server-side filtering without fetching all records, crucial for performance with large contact lists
evidence: CONT-001 L-002: Discovered bitwise search during Contact role implementation

sequence-number-vs-code-primary-key-patt.name: SequenceNumber vs Code Primary Key Strategy
sequence-number-vs-code-primary-key-patt.description: MoneyWorks tables use different primary key strategies: user-defined Code (Products) vs auto-generated SequenceNumber (Names, Contacts)
implications: Repository primaryKey must match table strategy
findByCode() only works for code-based tables
SequenceNumber tables require findBySequenceNumber() or filter queries
sequence-number-vs-code-primary-key-patt.rationale: Table key strategy affects repository design and query patterns
evidence: CONT-001 D-001: Contact uses SequenceNumber not Code

us-002-table-registry-validation.description: Verified Account appears in 'available' list after TableRegistry registration
evidence: AccountController registered at line 35 of table-registry.ts
